tossups.question: This man praised Bill Viola’s ability to “to bring the Californian forest into the theatre” as part of their co-production of Tristan und Isolde. The chorus wears contamination suits on a stage lit by a huge lightbulb in this man’s Chernobyl-inspired staging of Le Grand Macabre, which infuriated Gyorgy Ligeti. A repeated refrain in a libretto by this man mentions how “the cloud-flower blossoms” in each cardinal direction. A boombox replaces the wind ensemble in this man’s production of Don (*) Giovanni told as a blaxploitation story in the South Bronx. A libretto by this man features Pasqualita singing a Tewa song while the main character later quotes from “Batter my heart, three person'd God” at the Trinity test site. The libretto for Doctor Atomic was written by, for 10 points, what contemporary American opera director?
tossups.question_sanitized: This man praised Bill Viola's ability to "to bring the Californian forest into the theatre" as part of their co-production of Tristan und Isolde. The chorus wears contamination suits on a stage lit by a huge lightbulb in this man's Chernobyl-inspired staging of Le Grand Macabre, which infuriated Gyorgy Ligeti. A repeated refrain in a libretto by this man mentions how "the cloud-flower blossoms" in each cardinal direction. A boombox replaces the wind ensemble in this man's production of Don (*) Giovanni told as a blaxploitation story in the South Bronx. A libretto by this man features Pasqualita singing a Tewa song while the main character later quotes from "Batter my heart, three person'd God" at the Trinity test site. The libretto for Doctor Atomic was written by, for 10 points, what contemporary American opera director?

bonuses.leadin: The soprano Elizabeth Schwarzkopf compared many of this director’s choices to “spray[ing] graffiti on the Mona Lisa.” For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The soprano Elizabeth Schwarzkopf compared many of this director's choices to "spray[ing] graffiti on the Mona Lisa." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this theater and opera director who set a 1980 production of Handel’s Orlando in outer space. As a librettist, he has frequently worked with John Adams, such as on The Death of Klinghoffer.
In 1995, Sellars anachronistically included a machine gun and TVs in his Covent Garden staging of this composer’s opera about a Renaissance painter, Mathis der Maler.

Sellars’s production of this opera changed its setting to a penthouse on the 52nd floor of Trump Tower. Kirill Serebrennikov’s 2024 production of this opera in Berlin gave it a horror-thriller makeover.
parts_sanitized: Name this theater and opera director who set a 1980 production of Handel's Orlando in outer space. As a librettist, he has frequently worked with John Adams, such as on The Death of Klinghoffer.
In 1995, Sellars anachronistically included a machine gun and TVs in his Covent Garden staging of this composer's opera about a Renaissance painter, Mathis der Maler.

Sellars's production of this opera changed its setting to a penthouse on the 52nd floor of Trump Tower. Kirill Serebrennikov's 2024 production of this opera in Berlin gave it a horror-thriller makeover.
answers_sanitized: The Marriage of Figaro [or Le nozze de Figaro]

packet.name: D – Cambridge B, Rutgers A, Waterloo A, Yale A

bonuses.leadin: A Puccini-inspired opera by this composer is based on letters written during the California Gold Rush. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this composer of another opera in which the protagonist sings the “Aria of the Falling Body” as he is thrown overboard the Achille Lauro.
The aria “This is prophetic” is sung in the Summer Palace by this president’s wife Pat in another opera by Adams, which tells the story of his 1972 visit to China to visit Mao Zedong.
The original production of Nixon in China was produced by this theater director. This director also wrote the libretti for Adams’s operas Girls of the Golden West and Doctor Atomic.
answers: John Adams [or John Coolidge Adams] (The other opera is The Death of Klinghoffer.)
Richard Nixon [or Richard Milhous Nixon; accept Nixon in China]
answers_sanitized: John Adams [or John Coolidge Adams] (The other opera is The Death of Klinghoffer.)
Richard Nixon [or Richard Milhous Nixon; accept Nixon in China]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A Puccini-inspired opera by this composer is based on letters written during the California Gold Rush. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this composer of another opera in which the protagonist sings the "Aria of the Falling Body" as he is thrown overboard the Achille Lauro.
The aria "This is prophetic" is sung in the Summer Palace by this president's wife Pat in another opera by Adams, which tells the story of his 1972 visit to China to visit Mao Zedong.
The original production of Nixon in China was produced by this theater director. This director also wrote the libretti for Adams's operas Girls of the Golden West and Doctor Atomic.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about operas by John Adams, for 10 points each.
parts: Two answers required. These two characters title the most recent opera by Adams and were performed by Gerard Finley and Amina Edris to open the San Francisco Opera’s 100th season. Samuel Barber was commissioned to compose an opera about these two characters for the opening of the new Metropolitan Opera House.
This first opera by Adams begins with the arrival of a plane carrying the title president, whose wife receives a small glass elephant and sings the aria “This is prophetic” in the Summer Palace.

Adams has frequently collaborated with this theater director, who wrote the libretti for Doctor Atomic and Girls of the Golden West. This man also directed a production of The Marriage of Figaro set at Trump Tower.
answers: Antony AND Cleopatra [accept Mark Antony or Marc Anthony or Marcus Antonius in place of “Antony”; accept Cleopatra VII Philopator in place of “Cleopatra”]

packet.name: I – Brandeis A, Cambridge A, Cambridge B, Michigan A, NYU A

answers_sanitized: Antony AND Cleopatra [accept Mark Antony or Marc Anthony or Marcus Antonius in place of "Antony"; accept Cleopatra VII Philopator in place of "Cleopatra"]

bonuses.leadin: Inspired by this director’s staging of Messiaen’s opera Saint François d’Assise, Kaija Saariaho was inspired to write her first opera L’Amour de loin, which this man also directed. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this opera director. His best-known direction of an opera involved putting the presidential airplane ‘Spirit of ‘76’ and a giant bomb on the stage.

Sellars often directed and conceived operas by this composer, including writing the libretto for his opera Doctor Atomic. Sellars also directed this composer’s operas The Death of Klinghoffer and Nixon in China.

Sellars wrote another libretto for Adams’s most recent opera, titled for Girls of this specific two-word region. The title references a Puccini opera based on a David Belasco play titled for The Girl of this two-word region.
answers: Golden West [accept Girls or Girl of the Golden West, prompt on West]
answers_sanitized: Golden West [accept Girls or Girl of the Golden West, prompt on West]
